statement.markdown: You are given a recursive function to calculate the nth Fibonacci number with memoization. However, the function is not fully implemented and has a logical bug in the memoization part. Your task is to fix the bug and make the function compute the nth Fibonacci number correctly.
statement.inputFormat: The input is a single integer n, where 1 <= n <= 10000.
statement.outputFormat: Your function should print the nth Fibonacci number.
statement.notes: You can use static arrays or vectors to implement the memoization.

hints.text: Check your memoization logic and ensure you are storing and retrieving values correctly.

editorial.title: DP - Fibonacci Memoization
editorial.intuition: Memoization is a technique to store the results of expensive function calls so that we can reuse them when the same inputs occur again.
editorial.bruteForce: The naive recursive approach to calculate the nth Fibonacci number has a time complexity of O(2^n) due to repeated computations.
editorial.betterSolution: Memoization can reduce the time complexity to O(n) by storing previously computed values.
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement a DP approach with memoization to calculate the nth Fibonacci number.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the memoization approach can be proven by showing that it produces the same results as the naive recursive approach for all valid inputs.
editorial.dryRun: Step 1: Initialize a dynamic programming table of size n with all values set to 0.
